lessons.title: The Cell — Basic Unit of Life

content.body: All living things are made of cells. The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms. Some organisms like bacteria consist of only one cell (unicellular), while others like humans have trillions of cells (multicellular).

Cell Theory:
1. All living things are made of one or more cells
2. The cell is the basic unit of life
3. All cells come from pre-existing cells

There are two main types of cells: Prokaryotic cells — found in bacteria, they have no membrane-bound nucleus. Eukaryotic cells — found in plants, animals, and fungi, they have a true nucleus enclosed in a membrane.

content.title: Animal Cell Parts
content.body:
Cell membrane — controls what enters and leaves the cell
Nucleus — contains DNA and controls cell activities
Cytoplasm — jelly-like fluid where chemical reactions happen
Mitochondria — produces energy (ATP) through respiration
Ribosomes — where proteins are made
Endoplasmic reticulum — transports materials within the cell

content.title: Plant Cell — Extra Parts
content.body:
Cell wall — rigid outer layer made of cellulose, gives shape
Chloroplasts — contains chlorophyll for photosynthesis
Vacuole — large central space filled with cell sap
Plasma membrane — inside the cell wall
Plant cells differ from animal cells in three main ways: plant cells have a cell wall, chloroplasts, and a large permanent vacuole. Animal cells have none of these three features.

questions.question: What is the basic unit of life?
questions.explanation: The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms.

questions.question: Which organelle controls cell activities?
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains DNA and controls the activities of the cell.

questions.question: Where does energy production (respiration) occur?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are the powerhouse of the cell — they produce energy through respiration.

questions.question: Which structure is found in plant cells but NOT animal cells?
questions.explanation: Plant cells have a cell wall made of cellulose. Animal cells do not.

questions.question: What is the function of chloroplasts?
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and are the site of photosynthesis in plant cells.

questions.question: What does the cell membrane do?
options: Controls what enters and leaves the cell
questions.answer: Controls what enters and leaves the cell
questions.explanation: The cell membrane is selectively permeable — it controls what substances pass in and out.

questions.question: Which cells have no membrane-bound nucleus?
questions.explanation: Prokaryotic cells (like bacteria) have no nucleus surrounded by a membrane.

questions.question: Where are proteins made inside a cell?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are the site of protein synthesis in the cell.

questions.question: What fills the large vacuole in plant cells?
questions.explanation: The large central vacuole in plant cells is filled with cell sap (water and dissolved substances).

questions.question: Which statement is part of the Cell Theory?
options: All cells come from non-living matter
All living things are made of cells
questions.answer: All living things are made of cells
questions.explanation: Cell Theory states: all living things are made of cells, cells are the basic unit of life, and all cells come from pre-existing cells.
